threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' and have no clue about the bus routes that actually go anywhere.
Caught a guy serenading the night bus crew with a ukulele in Brixton last weekend. Oddly soothing as we rattled by empty streets.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Saw a kid on a bike pulling a mini grill behind him, shouting 'BBQ time!' to his friends. That’s when I knew Neukölln feels like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you see a random collection of doner shops holding court at a subway station entrance, each claiming to have the best secret sauce.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they sigh when the Muni is late—it's a mix of annoyance and acceptance, like it's a ritual.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy taking a nap on the 47 while nurses in scrubs squeezed in during rush hour—only in SF can the weary and the working coexist so closely.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the park, but miss the real vibe in the small plazas—it's where locals sip their café while kids play, far from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their unwrinkled metro maps—still pristine, unlike the folded, scribbled ones we all carry.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Overground' instead of just the 'Gooner', and you avoid the vegan cafes because you haven't figured out the gluten-free hype yet.
Caught a guy singing Adele at the back of the 333 last night. Everyone was awkwardly trying not to clap, but you could see some were tempted.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I truly lived in Neukölln when I saw an old man in a bathrobe chasing a runaway chicken down Warshawer Straße last summer.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Prenzlauer Berg will you find a spontaneous street wrestling match outside a café, complete with a DJ and a cheering crowd. Totally unexpected!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the bus schedule without checking their phone—it's like knowing the Muni routes by heart.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a woman bring her pet iguana on the 14 Mission last week. The driver didn’t bat an eye, just asked if it needed a seatbelt.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals are at the pessebre every holiday; meanwhile, we avoid it like the plague. It’s just too crowded now.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're fresh by their constant phone-checking while walking. Locals navigate with that innate sense of direction, no map needed.
